primitive.$description: Derived primitives — a palette's real colors, names derived from their oklch coordinates, descriptions generated from computed contrast under normal + deuteranopia/protanopia/tritanopia vision. No brand named; the name IS the color; the doc is computed from the value so it cannot drift.

oklch-24_09-0_0324-245_83-1.$description: A dark cool indigo. As text on light backgrounds it reaches 13.0 to 1 — past the 4.5:1 minimum for readable text — and still holds 12.5 to 1 for color-blind readers. So it stays legible for everyone, including the three common kinds of color-blindness.

oklch-19_44-0_0267-244_92-1.$description: A deep cool indigo. As text on light backgrounds it reaches 14.6 to 1 — past the 4.5:1 minimum for readable text — and still holds 14.1 to 1 for color-blind readers. So it stays legible for everyone, including the three common kinds of color-blindness.

oklch-34_01-0_0391-238_08-1.$description: A dark cool blue. As text on light backgrounds it reaches 9.3 to 1 — past the 4.5:1 minimum for readable text — and still holds 8.8 to 1 for color-blind readers. So it stays legible for everyone, including the three common kinds of color-blindness.

oklch-68_84-0_0479-236_32-1.$description: A light cool blue. As text on dark backgrounds it reaches 5.9 to 1 — past the 4.5:1 minimum for readable text — and still holds 5.8 to 1 for color-blind readers. So it stays legible for everyone, including the three common kinds of color-blindness.

oklch-95_17-0_0084-236_56-1.$description: An off-white. As text on dark backgrounds it reaches 14.2 to 1 — past the 4.5:1 minimum for readable text — and still holds 14.1 to 1 for color-blind readers. So it stays legible for everyone, including the three common kinds of color-blindness.

oklch-100-0-0-1.$description: A pure white. As text on dark backgrounds it reaches 16.4 to 1 — past the 4.5:1 minimum for readable text — and still holds 16.1 to 1 for color-blind readers. So it stays legible for everyone, including the three common kinds of color-blindness.

oklch-54_99-0_1251-52_03-1.$description: A mid-tone warm amber. As borders and dividers on light backgrounds it reaches 4.0 to 1 — past the 3:1 minimum for non-text — and still holds 3.7 to 1 for color-blind readers; As borders and dividers on dark backgrounds it reaches 3.2 to 1 — past the 3:1 minimum for non-text — and still holds 2.7 to 1 for color-blind readers. So it stays legible for everyone, including the three common kinds of color-blindness.

oklch-92_39-0_0336-73_19-1.$description: A very light warm amber. As text on dark backgrounds it reaches 13.0 to 1 — past the 4.5:1 minimum for readable text — and still holds 12.5 to 1 for color-blind readers. So it stays legible for everyone, including the three common kinds of color-blindness.
